title: Keyword Density Checker
shortDescription: Measure how often a keyword appears in a text and see its share of the total words.
metaDescription: Measure keyword frequency in any text and review counts, total words, and density in one place.
intro: Paste your text and the target keyword to see how often it appears, how many total words the text has, and the keyword’s density.
overview:
Use this tool when you need a clear count of a specific keyword inside an article, landing page, product description, or draft. It shows the keyword occurrences alongside the total word count so you can judge whether a term is used lightly, repeatedly, or too heavily.

The result is useful for editing content before publishing, comparing sections of the same page, or checking whether a phrase is being repeated in a way that sounds forced. It is especially helpful when the keyword has variants, punctuation around it, or appears in headings and body copy.
howItWorks: Paste or type the text you want to inspect.
Enter the keyword or phrase you want to count.
Review occurrences, total word count, and density in the result.

useCases.title: Review a blog draft
useCases.description: Check how often the main phrase appears in an article draft before you send it to an editor or publish it.

useCases.title: Balance a landing page
useCases.description: Compare the keyword use across a sales page so the message stays focused without sounding repetitive.

useCases.title: Audit product copy
useCases.description: See whether a product description repeats the same phrase too often or leaves the topic too vague.

useCases.title: Compare section by section
useCases.description: Test the introduction, body, and conclusion separately to find which part is driving the keyword count.

examples.title: Blog article review
examples.input:
Text: "Keyword density helps writers avoid repeating the same phrase too often. A keyword density checker can show whether keyword density is balanced."
Keyword: "keyword density"
examples.description: Shows how repeated phrase matches are counted inside a short article snippet.

examples.title: Product page audit
examples.input:
Text: "Buy running shoes for daily training. These running shoes are lightweight, durable, and comfortable running shoes for long distances."
Keyword: "running shoes"
examples.description: Useful for spotting repetition in sales copy where a phrase appears in several nearby sentences.

examples.title: Phrase with punctuation
examples.input:
Text: "Email marketing, email marketing strategies, and better email marketing results all matter."
Keyword: "email marketing"
examples.description: Demonstrates how a phrase is counted even when punctuation separates nearby mentions.

faq.question: Does the tool count exact words or partial matches too?
faq.answer: It counts the keyword or phrase you enter as it appears in the text. If you need singular and plural forms counted separately, test each version on its own.

faq.question: Why does the density change when I shorten the text?
faq.answer: Density depends on both keyword occurrences and total word count. If the text gets shorter while the keyword stays the same, the percentage rises.

faq.question: How should I read a very high density result?
faq.answer: A high percentage usually means the phrase appears many times in a short text. That can be fine in a short note, but it may sound repetitive in polished copy.

faq.question: What is a common mistake when checking a phrase?
faq.answer: A frequent mistake is entering only one word when the real target is a two- or three-word phrase. That gives a different count and can make the result misleading.

faq.question: Can I check different sections separately?
faq.answer: Yes. Pasting one section at a time is the best way to see where the keyword is concentrated instead of mixing the whole page together.

seo.title: Keyword Density Checker
seo.description: Measure keyword frequency in any text and review counts, total words, and density in one place.
